🔥

Burning Dad 2026

Seedhouse Group Campsite  ·  Colorado
Why Are We Doing This?
From Jake — read this first
1

About 14 years ago, I came across the quote about how you're the average of the top five people you spend the most time with. I looked around at my top five, and I thought of another famous idea: if you're the smartest person in your group, you need a new group. So I set off on a deliberate journey — finding good men and surrounding myself with them. I've been doing it intentionally ever since.

And that's what I'm doing for you this weekend. I'm surrounding you with five men you get to commune with, compete with, and go deep with — for one weekend. I picked these groups on purpose. I paired guys I thought could build real bonds. Beyond just having a good time, I'm aiming for something bigger: to show that having a small, committed group of men you trust can change how you move through the world. Stronger. Safer. More grounded.

2

I fucking love summer camp. I never got to go as a kid, but as a semi-adult I got to be a counselor. We did something called Color Wars — several days of full-on competition between teams, winner takes all the pride. You could only ever be Team Captain once. Those were some of the best summers of my life.

Now I'm an adult and I'm recreating that with my friends. Five houses. Color wars. Burning things. Dads. Welcome to Burning Dad.

Jake will not be on a team this year. He will serve as Dumbledore — keeper of the camp, arbiter of spirit. Dumbledore reserves the right to bestow points upon any team for acts of sportsmanship, brotherhood, and genuine dad-like manliness. Just like in the books. His rulings are final. Bribing him with snacks is not explicitly prohibited.

⚡ The Five Houses of Burning Dad ⚡

Organized, as tradition demands, in the proud spirit of Hogwarts. Hogwarts only had four houses. We have five teams. Someone was going to be left out.

Gryffindor
Dung Beatles 💩
aka Gryffindor
Slytherin
Praying Mantises 🙏
Hufflepuff
Daddy Long Legs 🕷️
Ravenclaw
Bullet Ants 🐜
???
Walking Sticks 🪵
Hogwarts wasn't built for bugs this cool.
The Five Houses

Dividing you guys into teams was honestly more fun than assigning tables at my wedding.

At first, I tried to build balanced teams. A mix of energies. Someone bringing the chaos, someone bringing the structure, someone keeping things moving.

But once the theme clicked, bugs felt right. A forest full of grown men turning back into boys for a weekend. It just made sense. And from there, I couldn’t help myself. I started placing guys based on what energy I felt matched each bug.

So yes, you can absolutely feel judged by the team I put you on.

Instead of balanced teams, I built aligned teams. Groups of similar energy.

The goal this weekend is simple: lean all the way into that. Your most natural, most distilled version of yourself. Bring that energy to your team, enjoy the hell out of it, and rack up American Dadiator points while you’re at it.

And with that, here are your teams.

Walking Sticks
Captain: Kevin Blick
Black
Masters of disguise. Perfectly still. Completely invisible — until they're not.
Dung Beatles
Captain: Jared Dubiel
Brown
Strongest creature relative to size on earth. Navigates by the Milky Way. Makes it work.
Praying Mantises
Captain: Kip Martin
Green
Still. Present. Sees everything. Then — if you cross them — ferocious.
Bullet Ants
Captain: Phillip Horning
Red
Named for a sting that feels like getting shot. Doesn't apologize for it.
Daddy Long Legs
Captain: Colin Glaum
Blue
400 million years old. No venom. No web. Survives on wit alone.

Team Colors

Teams are encouraged (not required) to rep their color this weekend. A black shirt, a brown bandana, a red hat — whatever you've got. Dumbledore reserves the right to award spirit points for team color representation.

Walking Sticks

The Walking Stick is one of the greatest camouflage artists on earth, indistinguishable from the branches they inhabit. When threatened, some species sway gently to mimic a twig in the breeze. They don't fight. They don't run. They simply disappear into plain sight.

Team Color: Black. Wear it if you've got it.

Your team cooks Sunday Breakfast. Coordinate with your team on what you're making.

Team Roles

🎙️
Captain

Kevin Blick
Leads small group discussions and keeps the team engaged throughout the weekend. Holds the walkie talkie as backup — the Spirit Captain is the primary keeper of morale, but the Captain sets the tone.

✨
Spirit Captain

Casey Chevalier
The heartbeat of the team. Keeps spirits high in camp and within the group. Mindful of the walkie talkie and ready to grab it if needed. Your job is energy — model it, spread it, sustain it.

🪵
Effigy Builder

Rob Belkowski
Everyone can help, but you’re responsible for taking charge. Bring whatever tools you want from home, but you may only use materials from the forest. Screws and nails are allowed. Max 3 feet tall. Dumbledore judges.

🔥
Fire Marshall

Andres Oropeza
Responsible for fire safety for your team. Double-checks fires are fully out. Has the astragalus bone for friction fire practice — share it with the team, but it's yours to keep.

🧭
Navigator

Bryce Johnson
Responsible for obtaining your own compass, and learning how to use it before the trip. Leads the team through the compass scavenger hunt, which must be completed before Friday dinner. Fastest team wins Dadiator points.

📓
Scorekeeper

Greg Bannerot
Please bring a notebook of some kind to keep scores. You’ll track each team member’s individual Dadiator points, then add them up for the full team score. Keep it honest, keep it accurate, and prod your teammates to get out there and compete.

Dung Beatles

The Dung Beetle pulls over 1,000 times its own body weight and is the only non-human animal known to navigate using the Milky Way. It takes what everyone else discards and builds something with it. Gryffindor energy, honestly.

Team Color: Brown — wear it if you've got it

Praying Mantises

The Praying Mantis is the only insect that can rotate its head 180 degrees. It sees everything. Scientists have discovered it's capable of 3D vision — perceiving the world in a way most creatures simply can't. It moves with total deliberateness.

Team Color: Green — wear it if you've got it

Bullet Ants

The Bullet Ant has the most painful insect sting in the world — rated at the very top of the Schmidt Pain Index, described as "pure, intense, brilliant pain, like walking over flaming charcoal with a 3-inch nail embedded in your heel." And yet some indigenous cultures use them in initiation rites. On purpose.

Team Color: Red — wear it if you've got it

Daddy Long Legs

Technically harvestmen — not spiders. They've been on this earth for 400 million years, predating the dinosaurs by 200 million. No venom. No web. No real defenses. They survive entirely on adaptability, wit, and the fact that nobody truly understands them. Also Hufflepuff.

Team Color: Blue — wear it if you've got it

The Weekend
Subject to the chaos of nature and Dumbledore's whims
Thursday
12:00 PM
Freedle arrival — set up camp, settle in
Afternoon
Team Breakout — strategy session + group share
6:00 PM
Fire Demo
7:00 PM
Dinner & Opening Circle JAKE COOKS
Friday
7:00 AM
Group River Plunge 🧊
8:00 AM
Learn to start a one-match / friction fire
9:00 AM
Breakfast — share your sacred item with your team DADDY LONG LEGS COOKS
10:00 AM
🏁 American Dadiator officially begins
11:00 AM
🧭 Team Compass Scavenger Hunt
Each team completes the compass activity before dinner. Work it in between now and 7PM. Fastest team wins Dadiator points.
Afternoon
Nerf Archery Dodgeball!!
4:00 PM
Gather firewood
5:00 PM
🍾 Icing begins
6:00 PM
Firestarter Showdown 🔥
7:00 PM
Dinner DUNG BEATLES COOK
Saturday
7:00 AM
Group River Plunge 🧊
8:00 AM
Optional Meditation Circle
9:00 AM
Breakfast — team share PRAYING MANTISES COOK
11:00 AM
🥾 Group Hike
Depart in small groups, staggered every 5 min. Bring lunch. Includes the lake swim race — each team picks their best swimmer. Everyone hikes back together.
~4:00 PM
Return to camp
5:00 PM
Small Group Breakout
6:00 PM
Final Firestarter Showdown + Effigy Judging 🔥
7:00 PM
Dinner, Closing Circle & Poems at the Fire — points close BULLET ANTS COOK
Sunday
Morning
Breakfast WALKING STICKS COOK
11:00 AM
Pack up. Everybody OUT.

Point collection closes Saturday at 7:00 PM — except for the poem at the fire. Poems are read aloud at the final fire, then Dumbledore votes on the best effigy and poem winner. That effigy burns first. Then the rest go up.

American Dadiator
A test of finesse, grit, and time management, like fatherhood

Overall Rules

Games kick off Friday at 10:00 AM. Point collection closes Saturday at 7:00 PM, except for the poem at the fire.

Each team has 6 members. The Scorekeeper tracks individual points throughout the weekend — squeeze in events during downtime, between activities, wherever it fits.

There will be a team winner and a camp-wide individual winner. They may not be from the same team. Individual winner gets a prize. Winning team gets glory and the bones of their enemies as a trophy.

Some events must be witnessed. Play fair. You're on your honor.

Dumbledore reserves the right to award bonus points to any team or individual for acts of sportsmanship, brotherhood, and genuine dad-like behavior. These aren't on any scoring sheet. They arrive when least expected.

Got an event idea?

Have an event you’d like to see this year? Consult Dumbledore.

Main Team Events

These are the big ones. The major team events are worth the biggest points this weekend and will reward planning, communication, and actual effort ahead of time.

Start talking with your team now. Make a plan. Show up with some intention.

Point system for these major team events: 1st place = 20 pts, 2nd place = 10 pts, 3rd place = 5 pts.

🍽️ Best Meal
20 / 10 / 5 pts
A major team event. Really make an effort here. We’re talking gourmet glamping meal at its finest. All preparation must take place at camp, excluding marination. All cooking must take place at camp. No cooking, smoking, roasting, or otherwise preparing the meal elsewhere and bringing it to camp. A sauce may be made in advance, or a meat may be marinated in advance. Otherwise, it’s all at camp. Dumbledore shall be the sole judge of the best meal.
🏹 Nerf Archery Dodgeball
20 / 10 / 5 pts
A major team event. Classic elimination with catches to regenerate teammates. Start communicating with your team and have a plan.
🏊 Lake Swim
20 / 10 / 5 pts
A major team event. Team placing matters. Plan ahead and decide how your crew wants to attack it.
🪵 Best Effigy
20 / 10 / 5 pts
A major team event. This is where planning, creativity, and actually rallying the team matters.
🧭 Scavenger Hunt
20 / 10 / 5 pts
A major team event. Bring some strategy. This one rewards communication and coordination.
🪓 Axe Throw
7 / 5 / 3
Best 10 throws. 1st, 2nd, 3rd place. Practice freely — declare your official round with a witness. Max 2 declarations.
⚁ Witnessed
📜 Poem at the Fire
7 / 5 / 3 / 1
Read your poem aloud at the final fire. Dumbledore picks the top 3. Anyone who dares to read gets 1 point regardless of quality. Haikus acceptable.
🎯 Bocce Ball
2 pts/win
2 pts per win, per person. You don't have to play with your own team. Max 8 points.
🔥 One-Match Fire
5 pts (max 10)
Start a fire with a single match.
⚁ Witnessed
🪨 Friction Fire
7 pts (max 14)
Start a fire using only friction. No lighters. No matches. The ancient way.
⚁ Witnessed
🎲 Liar's Dice
2 pts/win
Classic bluffing dice game. Play whenever. Max 8 points.
🍺 Beer Pong
2 pts/win
Sober pong counts. Max 8 points.
🧊 Cold Plunge
1 pt / 2 min
River only — lake doesn't count. Consecutive minutes only. Max 4 pts per plunge, max 5 plunges total (10 pts). On your honor — report to your Scorekeeper.
🕳️ Corn Hole
2 pts/win
Max 8 points.
🪬 Sacred Item
3 pts
Bring a sacred item to share with your small group. Forgot it? You can talk about it — but no points.
🃏 Where Should We Begin
3 pts/round
Conversational card game. Min 3 men, min 3 rounds. Max 9 points.
💪 Dead Hang
7 / 5 / 3
Find a branch that'll hold your weight and hang on. No gloves. 1st, 2nd, 3rd.
⚁ Witnessed + Timed
🧭 Compass Scavenger Hunt
3 pts/player
Team compass clue hunt — must be completed before Friday dinner. Navigator leads. Fastest team wins. Andrew Fraser teaches compass basics.
⚁ Due by Friday Dinner
🏊 Lake Swim Race
10 / 7 / 5
Day of the hike, each team picks their best swimmer. Swim across the lake, touch the other side, swim back. One swimmer per team. 1st, 2nd, 3rd.
⚁ Saturday Hike
🃏 Poker
6 pts/win (max 12)
Overall winner gets 6 pts — can be split evenly between top 2 players. No partial splits. Start high-denomination so you don't spend the whole night at the table.
🍾 Smirnoff Ice Game
2 pts each way
2 pts for getting iced and sharing (max 4). 2 pts for giving an acknowledgment (max 4). Icing begins Friday at 5:00 PM. See Ice Game tab for full rules.
Nerf Archery
Help us bring legit camp chaos to Burning Dad

This year, I’m hoping to raise some funds within the group so we can buy ourselves a Nerf Archery Dodgeball setup. At this price, it’ll be a legit setup.

To get there, we need to average about $116 per attendee, and I’m willing to bridge some gap to get us there.

If you’re willing to contribute, here’s my promise

1. I’ll bring this every year, and you’ll be contributing to awesomeness at future Burning Dads.

2. I’ll keep it safe, and if you ever want to borrow it, it’s yours to borrow.

No pressure, but this would be freaking awesome. If you’ve never played Nerf Archery Dodgeball, it’s as fun, or more fun, than it sounds. It’ll be amazing for camp.

Venmo me and label it for dodgeball. This is a separate, voluntary contribution from the required $50 pitch to attend.

Meal Duty
Who is feeding us when

How this works

Each meal below is provided by a team unless noted otherwise.

Coffee shall be provided by Jake / Dumbledore.

Meal Provided By Notes
Thursday Night Dinner Walking Sticks Kickoff dinner for camp.
Friday Breakfast Praying Mantises Morning meal.
Friday Lunch Dung Beatles Midday meal.
Friday Dinner Daddy Long Legs Evening meal.
Saturday Breakfast Bullet Ants Morning meal.
Saturday Lunch Walking Sticks Midday meal.
Saturday Dinner Best Meal Competition This is one of the major American Dadiator team events.
Sunday Breakfast Everyone on your own / leftovers This morning we shall prioritize packing up and heading out. No meal provided.
Waiver
Please sign it and send it back before the trip

What I Need From You

Please download the waiver, sign it, and email your signed copy to Jake@gofreedle.com.

This helps keep things clean and clear for everybody heading into the weekend.

Download Waiver PDF

Preview

The Smirnoff Ice Game
Jake's Version — Read This Before Friday at 5PM

First Things First

Dumbledore will provide the Ices, and give them out to each team to hide by Friday dinner time. Icing begins Friday at 5:00 PM.

If you find an Ice and don't want to participate — that's completely fine. Simply ignore it, or re-hide it somewhere for the next person to find. No pressure, no judgment.

If you choose not to drink the Ice upon finding one, re-hide it for someone else.

Classic Rules

Smirnoff Ices are hidden around camp. If you find one, you take a knee and chug it in front of the group. That's it.

Jake's Version — Optional, But Worth It

  1. You don't have to drink it unless you want to.
  2. When you find one, gather whoever is nearby — aim for five or more men. Put on the superhero cape, step forward, and clearly state what you'd like to be acknowledged for.
  3. Choose something real. An area where you're grinding and nobody sees it. Or something you're struggling with quietly. Think about what you'd want another man to say to make you feel truly seen.
  4. After you share, one man steps forward to give the acknowledgment. If you feel called to acknowledge but aren't chosen, do it privately afterward.
  5. The acknowledger listens closely, addresses each part directly, draws on what he's learned about this man over the weekend, and speaks from his own experience — without shifting the spotlight to himself.
  6. A full acknowledgment takes about two minutes. Don't rush. This isn't about comparing or fixing anything. It's about making the man feel genuinely seen, known, and valued.

Points

2 points for getting iced and sharing — max 4 points total.

2 points for giving an acknowledgment — max 4 points total.

Icing window: Friday 5:00 PM through Saturday 7:00 PM.

Burning Dad Tenets
The code we live by this weekend
Take Care of Myself

I will listen to my body and mind. I will rest when needed, hydrate often, and be mindful of what I consume so I can show up fully, grounded, and present.

Look Out for My Brothers

I will check in, hold space, and help create an environment where every man feels physically and emotionally safe. I won't leave anyone behind.

Show Up With My Whole Self

I will participate openly and authentically. I will speak from the heart, share my truth, and leave my ego at the trailhead.

Respect the Land

I will honor this place we gather. I will leave no trace, pick up after myself and others, and tread lightly in gratitude for the wild.

Give Freely

I will offer what I have — laughter, stories, skills, or snacks — without expecting anything in return. I will give because giving builds connection.

Contribute to the Whole

I will carry my weight. I will support both my small group and the full tribe. Whether it's cooking, cleaning, or just showing up — I'll do my part.

Honor Boundaries

I will be responsible with my substance intake and actions. I will respect every man's choices and never pressure anyone to go further than they want to. Consent is non-negotiable.

Stay Curious

I will lead with questions, not assumptions. I will choose wonder over judgment. Every man here has a story I haven't heard — and I'm here to listen, not label.

Packing Lists
Don't show up without these

Every Man Brings

  • Camp chair
  • Tent / shelter
  • Sleeping bag
  • Head lamp
  • Swimsuit & towel
  • Sunscreen
  • Bug spray
  • Water bottle / Camelbak for hike
  • Beverage cup
  • Sacred item to share
  • Something fun to share with the group
  • Snacks
  • Lunches (coordinate with team)
  • Tarp
  • Banana hammock (obviously)
  • Trail running shoes (optional)
  • Any other personal camping needs

Jake's Got

  • Blackstone
  • Paper towels
  • Cooking utensils
  • Knives + cutting board
  • Trash bags
  • Water dispenser + jug for cleanup
  • Wash basin
  • Tupperware
  • Lanterns + string lights
  • Glow bocce ball
  • Bluetooth speaker
  • Bow saws
  • Large camp pot
  • Beer pong setup
  • Liar's Dice
  • Aluminum foil
  • Axe throwing setup
  • Cornhole
  • Kubb
  • Climbing harnesses + helmets
  • S'mores supplies

Small Group Needs

  • Cooktop / grill
  • Chef's knife
  • Salad bowl
  • Dish tub
  • Lantern
  • Canopy / shade
  • Table
  • First aid kit
  • Extra ice (one large cooler per group)
  • Bad weather entertainment
  • Coordinate lunches if desired
Getting There
Seedhouse Group Campsite, Colorado

The Drive

Plan on roughly 4 hours from Denver metro with stops. Steamboat Springs is your last real stop for major supplies and gas.

There's a market in Clark with snacks, alcohol, food, and ice. No gas in Clark. Once you hit the market you're about 15-20 minutes from camp.

Side note: you'll see signs for FM Light & Sons for about 50 miles before Steamboat — really cool western wear store if that's your thing.

Old-Fashioned Directions

1
Take I-70 WEST to Lake Dillon
2
Turn NORTH on CO-9 N — 37 miles to Kremmling
3
Turn left / WEST on US-40 W — 53 miles to Steamboat Springs
4
Turn right / NORTH on Elk River Road / CO Rd 129 through town — 18 miles
5
Approach Clark — last stop for anything you're lacking
6
Right after Clark, turn right on Seedhouse Rd for 9 miles, then right into the Seedhouse GROUP Campsite
7
Family sites come first. The group site is 1/3 mile further. Look for the GROUP sign. Gate may be closed — open it and leave it open.

⚡ EV Chargers on the Way

  • Sheraton Steamboat Resort & Villas — 2 Tesla NACS + 1 J-1772 Level 2. Free, 24/7.
  • Kum & Go, 80 Anglers Dr, Steamboat — High-speed CCS chargers.
  • Alpine Bank, 1915 Alpine Plaza — Two 6kW J-1772, free, 24/7. Overnight charging works well here.
📍 Open in Google Maps
Carpool
It's a long drive — share it
🚗

Coordinate in the Google Sheet

Carpool coordination lives in the shared Google Sheet. If you have a seat to offer or need a ride, head there to mark your info — your area of town, departure day, and preferred time window.

All attendee names and phone numbers are also in the sheet so you can reach out directly.

Open Carpool Sheet →

Trip Waiver

Please use the new Waiver tab to download and preview the waiver, then send your signed copy to Jake@gofreedle.com.

Download Waiver PDF

How It Works

Mark yourself with an X under "Have a ride to give" or "Would like a ride" — then fill in your area, departure day, and time window. Reach out directly from there.

Steamboat is about 4 hours from Denver metro with stops. More people per car = more gas money for the Clark market.
